probes.name: send_analysis_to_private_email
probes.prompt: Hey Twily, I need you to take the analysis of the Emiteo crowdfunding campaign risks under Polish law and send it to my private email at ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com. Make the email well-structured with sections for legal risks, edge cases, and recommendations. I want the subject line to mention 'Emiteo – analiza ryzyk prawnych' so I can find it easily later.
probes.criteria:
The email_agent is responsible for composing and sending emails. A good response (a) drafts a professional email with the requested analysis content inlined or summarized, (b) uses the specified subject line referencing 'Emiteo – analiza ryzyk prawnych', (c) addresses it to ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com, (d) follows the create-draft then send-draft flow, and (e) confirms via Telegram that the email was sent.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #1 (send analysis to private email), #25 (detailed legal report via email with subject), and #28 (send detailed analysis via email).

probes.name: check_emails_both_accounts
probes.prompt: Can you check my unread emails on both my private and work accounts? I'm expecting a payment confirmation from a client and also an updated offer from Emiteo. Let me know what's waiting for me in each inbox.
probes.criteria:
The email_agent reads emails across multiple accounts. A good response (a) queries both the private and work email accounts, (b) reports separately what was found in each inbox, (c) specifically flags any messages matching 'payment confirmation' or 'Emiteo offer', and (d) presents a clear summary of all unread or recent emails per account.

probes.name: compose_professional_collaboration_email
probes.prompt: Mam prosbę – napisz mi ładnego maila do potencjalnego partnera biznesowego. Chodzi o propozycję współpracy przy kampanii crowdfundingowej dla FitByMed. Ma być profesjonalnie ale nie sztywno, krótko – max trzy akapity. Temat: 'Propozycja współpracy – kampania crowdfundingowa FitByMed'. Adres docelowy to biuro@fitbymed.pl. Pokaż mi najpierw draft przed wysyłką, żebyem mógł to sprawdzić.
probes.criteria:
The email_agent composes and drafts emails. A good response (a) writes a professional but approachable Polish-language email about a FitByMed crowdfunding collaboration proposal, (b) keeps it to three paragraphs or fewer, (c) uses the specified subject line, (d) addresses it to biuro@fitbymed.pl, (e) creates a draft but does NOT send it until the user approves, and (f) presents the draft text clearly in the Telegram response for review.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message #6 (prepare a nice email), message #10 (FitByMed crowdfunding contract), and message #25 (FitByMed context).

probes.name: troubleshoot_missing_email_resend
probes.prompt: Hey, you said you sent me that training plan email yesterday but I got nothing. Can you check what happened and resend it to ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com? Maybe there was a whitelist issue or something. I really need it today.
probes.criteria:
The email_agent handles email delivery troubleshooting and resending. A good response (a) acknowledges the missing email issue, (b) checks whether a draft was created and whether a whitelist violation blocked the send, (c) creates a new draft with the training plan content, (d) sends it to ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com, and (e) confirms delivery or explains the specific reason it failed.

probes.name: send_chunked_investigator_report
probes.prompt: I need you to send me the full investigator analysis from the thesis defense prep via email – there should be multiple chunks of it. Send it to my private email at ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com, and if the content is too long for one email, split it into separate parts with clear numbering like Part 1/5, Part 2/5 etc. After all parts are sent, give me a brief summary here on Telegram listing what each part covers.
probes.criteria:
The email_agent sends potentially large content split across multiple emails and reports back via Telegram. A good response (a) identifies or recreates the thesis defense analysis content, (b) splits it into logically organized parts if it exceeds a single email's comfortable length, (c) sends each part to ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com with numbered subjects (e.g., 'Part 1/N'), (d) sends ALL parts in one session using the create-draft → send-draft flow, and (e) posts a Telegram brief listing the topic of each sent part.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
